comps.rationale: Major railcar manufacturer and lessor; Trinity's leasing arm is a direct comp for rail asset-backed lending vehicles like AAM's intermodal feeder.

profile.business_description: AAM Series 1.1 Rail and Domestic Intermodal Feeder, LLC is a feeder fund within the Amergin Asset Management platform that invests in rail cars, locomotives, and domestic intermodal containers and chassis. The entity provides asset-backed financing for the acquisition and leasing of railroad and intermodal transportation equipment.
profile.end_customers: The primary end customers are Class I railroads, short-line railroad operators, intermodal carriers, and shipping companies that lease rail cars, intermodal containers, and chassis rather than purchasing them outright. Industrial shippers of bulk commodities, chemicals, and manufactured goods that require specialized rail car fleets are the ultimate users of the leased equipment.

Airlines serving national and international route networks benefit from Amergin's sale-and-leaseback and financing lease structures to support their fleet requirements.

Founded in 2001 and acquired by Bain Capital in 2021, the company connects providers with mission-critical replacement parts and service for medical devices and hospital equipment through a technology-enabled procurement platform.

profile.business_description: Buildertrend provides cloud-based construction project management software that connects home builders, remodelers, and specialty contractors with their clients and trade partners throughout the building process. profile.end_customers: Buildertrend serves over 20,000 residential construction businesses primarily in the United States, powering over half of new home builds in the country. Its core customers are custom home builders, remodelers, and specialty contractors who manage multiple projects and crews, along with the homeowners and subcontractors who interact with the platform for approvals, selections, and scheduling.

profile.business_description: Valenz Health provides an end-to-end healthcare ecosystem optimization platform that manages cost and quality of care for self-insured employers through payment integrity, clinical engagement, out-of-network repricing, and price transparency solutions. The company's VMS repricing methodology delivers an average 67% savings on out-of-network claims with less than a 1% appeal rate.
profile.end_customers: Valenz's primary end-customers are small and mid-sized self-insured employers seeking to reduce healthcare spending, accessed through a channel of third-party administrators, carriers, and brokers who embed Valenz's solutions into their benefits administration. The company also directly serves health plans and stop-loss carriers that need payment integrity, utilization management, and cost containment services.

profile.business_description: Blend Labs (NYSE: BLND) is a digital lending and banking platform that powers consumer lending origination for banks, credit unions, and mortgage lenders, processing $1.2 trillion in loan applications in 2024. profile.end_customers: Blend's primary end customers are banks, credit unions, and mortgage lenders that license its platform to digitize and streamline their consumer lending origination processes. The ultimate beneficiaries are the individual borrowers and consumers who experience faster, simpler digital mortgage applications, home equity loans, and deposit account openings through Blend-powered lender websites and apps.

profile.end_customers: Catalent's end-customers are pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies of all sizes that outsource drug formulation development, clinical-stage manufacturing, and commercial-scale production. The company has supported more than half of FDA-approved products over the past decade, serving customers that need biologics fill-finish, softgel encapsulation, modified-release formulations, and global clinical supply chain management.

investments.sponsor_source: Novo Holdings (owned by Novo Nordisk Foundation) acquired Catalent for $16.5B via Creek Parent Inc. in December 2024

profile.business_description: New Relic provides a cloud-based observability platform that enables engineering teams to monitor application performance, infrastructure, and user experiences across their full technology stack. The platform collects telemetry data (metrics, events, logs, and traces) via lightweight agents and provides real-time dashboards, alerting, distributed tracing, and AI-driven incident detection.
profile.end_customers: New Relic's end-customers are software engineering and DevOps teams at companies of all sizes, from startups to large enterprises, who need to monitor the performance and reliability of their applications and infrastructure. The platform is used across industries including technology, financial services, media, and e-commerce by organizations running cloud-native, hybrid, or on-premises environments.

profile.business_description: Datavant is the leading health data connectivity platform that enables the secure linking and exchange of de-identified patient data across the healthcare ecosystem using patented tokenization technology. The platform enables more than 60 million healthcare records to move between thousands of organizations, connecting over 80,000 hospitals and clinics, 75% of the 100 largest health systems, and 350+ real-world data partners.
profile.end_customers: Datavant's end-customers are health plans, health systems, life sciences companies, and real-world data analytics firms that need to link and analyze patient-level data across disparate sources while maintaining HIPAA compliance. The platform also serves clinical research organizations, legal and insurance firms, and government agencies that need de-identified data connectivity for outcomes research, regulatory submissions, and population health analysis.

investments.sponsor_source: New Mountain Capital led the combination of Ciox Health and Datavant in 2021 to form the largest health data ecosystem

profile.business_description: Rectangle Health provides an all-in-one patient engagement, payment, and compliance platform called Practice Management Bridge, serving over 40,000 healthcare organizations and 161,000 users. Founded in 1993, the platform captures more than $9.5 billion in annual patient payment volume and sends over 70 million patient communications per month, integrating scheduling, appointment reminders, card-on-file payments, patient financing, and HIPAA/OSHA compliance management.
profile.end_customers: Rectangle Health's end-customers are healthcare practices and provider organizations across the care spectrum, including medical, dental, and specialty practices that need to streamline patient payments, reduce accounts receivable, and automate front-office operations. The platform serves practice administrators and billing teams who report a 97% in-office collection rate and 47% decrease in outstanding AR after adoption.

comps.rationale: Direct functional comp as enterprise content/information management platform; publicly traded with both loans and bonds. Divested AMC unit to Rocket Software (2024).

comps.name: Flexera Software
comps.debt: $3B private credit (KKR-led Aug 2025): $2.03B TL + $689M EUR TL + $150M revolver, all at S+475/E+475. Thoma Bravo-owned. Replaced prior $1.95B BSL due 2028.
comps.rationale: Thoma Bravo-owned enterprise software (IT asset mgmt); same PE sponsor as Hyland with comparable scale and recently refinanced debt.

profile.business_description: Hyland provides enterprise content management (ECM) and content services software that helps organizations capture, manage, and automate document-centric workflows. Its product portfolio includes OnBase (on-premises ECM), Alfresco (open-source content management), and Nuxeo (cloud-native content platform), serving complex document and records management needs.
profile.end_customers: Hyland serves more than half of the Fortune 100, with primary end-customers in healthcare (hospitals and health systems for medical records and claims processing), government (approximately 1,500 agencies for public records and case management), financial services (insurance companies and banks), and higher education institutions. The software is used by operations, compliance, and IT teams that manage high volumes of documents and regulatory records.

investments.origination_notes: Maturity 1/2030 minus 7yr tenor. Present in 2024 portfolio

comps.name: Coupa (Coupa Holdings)

comps.rationale: Both are PE-backed enterprise SaaS platforms taken private at scale. Similar software-focused PE LBO profile with syndicated leveraged loans trading in secondary markets.

comps.name: Avalara
comps.debt: $2.5B recurring-revenue TL (Blue Owl-led). Vista Equity $8.4B take-private (2022). Traded ~94.75 in secondary.

comps.name: Athenahealth
comps.debt: $5.9B TLB (SOFR+350, 50bp floor, 99.5 OID) + $1B DDTL. H&F/Bain $17B LBO (2022). Broadly syndicated, actively traded.
comps.rationale: Large PE-backed SaaS platform (healthcare payments/engagement). Athenahealth's broadly syndicated TLB is one of the most liquid software credits, providing pricing benchmark for payment-adjacent SaaS.

profile.business_description: EngageSmart is a provider of vertically tailored customer engagement software and integrated payment solutions, acquired by Vista Equity Partners for $4 billion in 2024. The company operates SaaS platforms including SimplePractice (health and wellness practice management), InvoiceCloud (utility and government bill payment), and DonorDrive (fundraising), all designed to drive digital adoption and self-service.
profile.end_customers: EngageSmart served approximately 119,600 customers across its SMB and Enterprise segments as of its acquisition, spanning core verticals of health and wellness, government, utilities, financial services, and charitable giving. Its end users are consumers and businesses interacting through these vertical platforms--patients booking health appointments, utility customers paying bills, and donors contributing to fundraising campaigns.

profile.business_description: Ideal Image is North America's largest medical aesthetics provider, founded in 2001 and headquartered in Tampa, Florida, with over 20 million FDA-cleared treatments performed across its nationwide clinic network. The company offers laser hair removal, body sculpting (CoolSculpting), Botox and filler injections, skin rejuvenation treatments, and medical-grade skincare through licensed medical professionals.
profile.end_customers: Ideal Image primarily serves women aged 25-55 seeking non-invasive cosmetic and aesthetic treatments at accessible price points, though its customer base increasingly includes men. Clients are typically middle-income consumers looking for medically supervised alternatives to surgical cosmetic procedures, accessed through the company's medspa clinics across the U.S. and Canada.

profile.business_description: Innovation Ventures, through its subsidiary Living Essentials LLC, manufactures and markets 5-hour Energy, the leading energy shot brand in the United States. The two-ounce liquid shot contains a blend of B-vitamins, amino acids, and caffeine comparable to a cup of coffee, with zero sugar and low calories. Founded by Manoj Bhargava and launched in 2004, the product generated over $1 billion in annual revenue by 2011.
profile.end_customers: 5-hour Energy targets on-the-go adults seeking a quick, portable energy boost, including truck drivers, office workers, students, and athletes. The product is sold primarily through convenience stores, gas stations, grocery stores, and mass-market retailers across the United States.

profile.business_description: Intelerad is a global leader in medical imaging software, providing cloud-based PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System), enterprise imaging, and radiology workflow solutions to nearly 2,500 healthcare organizations worldwide. The company's IntelePACS platform supports high-volume diagnostic radiology with tools for PET-CT fusion, multi-modality comparison, and AI integration, and GE HealthCare has announced an agreement to acquire Intelerad for $2.3 billion.

The company provides point-of-sale data, consumer panel insights, and advanced analytics through its Liquid Data technology platform, covering consumer packaged goods, durable goods, foodservice, and retail industries.
profile.end_customers: Circana serves nearly 7,000 brand manufacturers and retailers worldwide who rely on its market data and analytics to understand consumer purchasing behavior, optimize pricing, track market share, and inform product development decisions. Its clients span CPG companies, consumer electronics manufacturers, apparel brands, toy makers, foodservice operators, and major retailers seeking competitive intelligence and demand forecasting.

profile.business_description: Jama Software provides Jama Connect, a requirements management and product development platform that enables teams to define, manage, and trace requirements throughout the development lifecycle while auto-detecting development risks. The platform is particularly strong in regulated industries where traceability from requirements through verification and validation is mandated by standards like ISO 14971 and IEC 62304.
profile.end_customers: Jama Connect serves over 12.5 million users across 30 countries, primarily in highly regulated industries including aerospace and defense, medical devices, automotive, semiconductor, life sciences, and industrial manufacturing. Notable customers include NASA, Boeing, Caterpillar, and Rockwell Collins--organizations where rigorous requirements traceability and change management are essential for product safety and regulatory compliance.

profile.business_description: Klarna is a Swedish fintech company and the world's leading buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) provider, allowing consumers to split purchases into interest-free installments at over 500,000 retail partners worldwide. The company went public on the NYSE in September 2025 in a $1.37 billion IPO and has expanded beyond BNPL into digital banking, the Klarna Card, and AI-powered shopping tools serving 111 million consumers globally.
profile.end_customers: Klarna's primary end customers are online and in-store consumers—particularly millennials and Gen Z shoppers—who use its pay-in-4 installment plans, financing options, and price comparison tools at checkout. The company also serves e-commerce retailers and brands who integrate Klarna at checkout to increase conversion rates, average order values, and customer acquisition.

profile.business_description: Loparex is a global leader in the manufacture of release liners, which are silicone-coated papers and films used as protective carriers or process aids in pressure-sensitive adhesive applications. The company operates manufacturing facilities across North America, Europe, and Asia, producing release liners in a wide range of substrates and coatings.
profile.end_customers: Loparex's primary end customers are manufacturers of pressure-sensitive labels, tapes, graphic arts materials, medical products, hygiene products, and industrial composites. Major consumer brands and converters across the packaging, healthcare, and building materials industries rely on Loparex release liners as a critical input in their manufacturing processes.

investments.sponsor_source: Pamplona Capital Management acquired Loparex from Intermediate Capital Group (ICG) in 2019

Pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies are the direct counterparties to the structured financing provided by this Irish-domiciled entity.

profile.business_description: Lytx is a leading provider of video telematics and fleet management solutions, offering AI-powered dash cameras, driver safety analytics, and fleet tracking services for commercial vehicle fleets. The company's DriveCam platform uses machine vision and artificial intelligence to detect risky driving behaviors and provide real-time coaching alerts to drivers.
profile.end_customers: Lytx's primary end customers are commercial fleet operators across trucking, transportation, field services, public transit, and government fleets that seek to reduce accidents, lower insurance costs, and improve driver safety. Logistics companies, utility fleets, and school bus operators are among the customer segments deploying Lytx cameras and telematics across their vehicle fleets.

profile.business_description: Maia Aggregator, LP is a co-investment aggregator vehicle that pools capital from multiple investors to participate alongside a lead private equity sponsor in a specific healthcare or life sciences portfolio company investment. Limited public information is available about the specific underlying portfolio company, as co-investment aggregators are structured to provide additional equity capital for large leveraged buyouts.
profile.end_customers: As a co-investment vehicle, Maia Aggregator has no direct end-customers; it exists to hold a passive equity interest in an underlying healthcare portfolio company. The specific end-customers depend on the operating company in which the vehicle has invested, which is not publicly disclosed.

profile.business_description: Maple Acquisition is the holding entity for Medicus Healthcare Solutions, a leading locum tenens staffing firm that places physicians and advanced practice providers in temporary assignments at healthcare facilities nationwide. Founded in 2004 and headquartered in Windham, New Hampshire, Medicus is the fourth-largest locum tenens staffing firm in the United States with over 300 employees, backed by BPOC.
profile.end_customers: Medicus's end-customers are hospitals, health systems, physician group practices, and federally qualified health centers that need temporary physician and advanced practitioner coverage to fill staffing gaps due to vacations, leaves of absence, recruiting delays, or volume surges. The company serves healthcare facilities across medical specialties that require credentialed clinicians for interim assignments ranging from days to months.

profile.business_description: Milan Laser is the largest laser hair removal company in the United States, founded in 2012 in Omaha, Nebraska, now operating 300+ clinics across 30 states with over 1,600 employees. The company exclusively performs laser hair removal treatments under medical supervision, differentiating itself with an Unlimited Package that covers unlimited treatments for life at no additional cost.
profile.end_customers: Milan Laser serves individual consumers -- predominantly women, though increasingly men -- seeking permanent laser hair removal across all skin tones. Clients typically range from young adults to middle-aged professionals willing to pay for the convenience and long-term savings of permanent hair reduction versus ongoing shaving or waxing.

profile.business_description: Mindbody provides cloud-based business management software for the wellness and fitness industry, offering scheduling, point-of-sale, client management, marketing, payroll, and a consumer marketplace app. The platform connects wellness businesses with consumers through the Mindbody app, which serves as a discovery and booking marketplace with over 2.8 million active users.
profile.end_customers: Mindbody serves approximately 19,000 wellness and fitness businesses including yoga and pilates studios, gyms, personal training centers, spas, salons, massage centers, dance studios, and wellness centers. The consumer-facing Mindbody app also makes end-consumers a key customer segment, as they use the marketplace to discover, book, and pay for fitness and wellness services.

profile.business_description: Minerva Holdco is the holding company structure through which Hellman & Friedman and Bain Capital jointly acquired athenahealth Group in a $17 billion take-private transaction completed in 2022. athenahealth is a cloud-based provider of electronic health records (EHR), revenue cycle management, patient engagement, care coordination, and population health services for the healthcare industry.
profile.end_customers: athenahealth's end-customers are medical practices, hospitals, and health systems that use its cloud platform for clinical documentation, claims management, patient communications, and quality reporting. The company serves providers ranging from small independent practices to large health systems, with particular strength in ambulatory care settings where physicians need integrated EHR, billing, and patient engagement tools.

comps.debt: ~$3B+ syndicated debt (1st-lien + 2nd-lien), Clearlake $5.2B LBO (2021). Arranged by JP Morgan, BofA, Ares, Golub, and others.

comps.name: Kaseya
comps.debt: $2.95B 7-year 1st-lien TLB (SOFR+325) + $925M 2nd-lien TLB. Broadly syndicated refinancing (2025), led by Morgan Stanley. Insight Partners-backed.
comps.rationale: Same sponsor (Insight Partners) as Ministry Brands. Broadly syndicated leveraged loans trade in secondary markets. Provides direct comp for Insight-sponsored software credit pricing.

profile.business_description: Ministry Brands is the leading provider of SaaS management platforms purpose-built for churches, parachurch ministries, nonprofits, and other faith-based organizations in North America. The platform offers church management, online giving and donation processing, accounting, member engagement, background screening, and website services, processing over $6 billion in donations and nearly 2 million background checks annually.
profile.end_customers: Ministry Brands serves over 90,000 faith-based and nonprofit organizations across North America, managing approximately 10 million members. Its customers include individual churches of all denominations, Catholic parishes and dioceses (over 9,000 parishes and 185 arch/dioceses use its ParishSOFT product), and other religious and nonprofit institutions.

profile.business_description: ModMed (Modernizing Medicine) provides specialty-specific, cloud-based electronic health record (EHR) systems, practice management, billing, revenue cycle management, and patient engagement tools built by and for specialist physicians. The company's EMA platform is ranked #1 across its specialties and features an adaptive learning engine and AI-powered ambient scribe technology, serving dermatology, ophthalmology, orthopedics, gastroenterology, ENT, urology, pain management, and plastic surgery practices.
profile.end_customers: ModMed's end-customers are specialty physician practices ranging from solo practitioners to large multi-site groups that need EHR systems tailored to their clinical workflows rather than generic primary care platforms. profile.business_description: National Dentex (NDX) is the largest network of fully-owned dental laboratories in North America, operating 55+ full-service labs with nearly 4,000 employees and generating approximately $750 million in annual revenue, backed by Cerberus Capital Management. The company provides a complete range of dental prosthetics and restorative products including crowns, bridges, dentures, implants, surgical guides, and orthodontic appliances.
profile.end_customers: National Dentex's end-customers are tens of thousands of dentists, dental specialists (prosthodontists, oral surgeons, orthodontists), and dental service organizations (DSOs) that send dental impressions and digital scans to NDX labs for fabrication of crowns, bridges, implant restorations, dentures, and other prosthetic devices. The labs ultimately serve dental patients who need restorative or prosthetic dental work.

profile.business_description: NMI (Network Merchants, Inc.) is a global embedded payments platform powering over $225 billion in annual payment volumes, providing payment gateway, merchant acquiring, underwriting, onboarding, and management solutions. The company offers a unified, white-label payment acceptance platform that can be embedded within software and technology stacks for omnichannel commerce.
profile.end_customers: NMI's primary end customers are independent sales organizations (ISOs), independent software vendors (ISVs), and payment facilitators who embed NMI's payment technology into their own platforms and resell it to merchants. profile.business_description: Nscale is a vertically integrated AI cloud infrastructure provider that operates owned and colocated data centers with large-scale GPU clusters for AI training and inference workloads. The company has secured partnerships with NVIDIA, Microsoft, and OpenAI, and is building massive AI campuses across the UK, Norway (Stargate Norway with 100,000 GPUs), and the US (240MW Texas facility), with a recent $2 billion raise at a $14.6 billion valuation.
profile.end_customers: Nscale's end customers are enterprises, AI research labs, and hyperscale technology companies that require large-scale GPU compute for training and deploying AI models. Key customers and partners include Microsoft and OpenAI, while the platform also serves a broader base of AI startups and enterprises through its serverless inference endpoints, on-demand training clusters, and private cloud environments.

profile.business_description: PetVet Care Centers is one of the largest veterinary hospital networks in the United States, owning and operating approximately 450 general practice, emergency/specialty, and equine hospitals across 39 states. The company serves companion animal owners through its general practice and emergency hospitals, and equestrian clients through its equine veterinary facilities.

profile.business_description: PathGroup is a premier provider of anatomic, clinical, and molecular pathology services headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, founded in 1965. The company employs more than 240 pathologists working across two major laboratory facilities (a 140,000 sq ft Nashville lab and a 135,000 sq ft Dallas lab) and operates 24/7, using advanced digital pathology and AI to deliver diagnostic results.
profile.end_customers: PathGroup serves more than 250 hospitals and 15,000 referring physicians across the United States who outsource pathology testing and diagnostic interpretation. The company is also one of the largest providers of women's health pathology services, serving OB/GYN practices and patients who need Pap smear analysis, HPV testing, and related diagnostics.

profile.end_customers: Winland Foods primarily serves grocery retailers and food companies seeking private label and co-pack manufacturing partners for center-of-store products. Its end consumers are everyday grocery shoppers purchasing store-brand and branded pasta, sauces, syrups, and other shelf-stable food items through retail channels.

investments.sponsor_source: TreeHouse Foods sold its Meal Preparation business to Investindustrial for $950M; the divested business was renamed Winland Foods. Investindustrial later merged it with La Doria to form Windoria

profile.business_description: Securonix provides a cloud-native, AI-reinforced Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platform that unifies security analytics, threat intelligence, investigation, and automated response capabilities. Recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for SIEM six consecutive times, the platform specializes in insider threat detection, user and entity behavior analytics (UEBA), and cloud security monitoring.
profile.end_customers: Securonix serves large enterprises, financial institutions, government agencies, and managed security service providers (MSSPs) that need advanced threat detection and security operations capabilities. Its customers include banks, oil and gas companies, and other organizations in highly regulated industries where security operations center (SOC) teams require behavior-based analytics to detect insider threats, account compromise, and advanced persistent threats.

profile.business_description: Humanetics is the global leader in the design and manufacture of crash test dummies (anthropomorphic test devices) and safety measurement sensors used in automotive safety testing. The company also develops advanced simulation software and active safety testing systems used to validate vehicle crashworthiness and occupant protection systems.
profile.end_customers: Humanetics' primary end customers are global automotive OEMs, automotive safety testing laboratories, and government regulatory agencies (such as NHTSA and Euro NCAP) that use crash test dummies to evaluate vehicle safety performance. Tier 1 automotive suppliers developing airbags, seat belts, and restraint systems also purchase Humanetics' instrumented test devices and simulation tools.

investments.sponsor_source: Bridgepoint acquired Humanetics (Safety Technology Holdings) from Golden Gate Capital in 2018

profile.business_description: SimpliSafe is a home security company founded in 2006 in Boston, Massachusetts, that designs, manufactures, and sells wireless DIY home security systems with optional 24/7 professional monitoring. The company's product lineup includes a base station, entry sensors, motion sensors, glass break detectors, water sensors, security cameras, smoke detectors, and panic buttons, all connected via cellular and Wi-Fi with no long-term contracts required.
profile.end_customers: SimpliSafe serves over 5 million homeowners and apartment renters across the United States who want affordable, self-installed home security without long-term contracts or professional installation fees. The company particularly appeals to tech-savvy consumers, first-time homeowners, and renters who value flexibility and straightforward pricing.

profile.business_description: PowerSchool is the leading provider of cloud-based software for K-12 education in North America, supporting over 50 million students and 15,000+ school district customers globally. Founded in 1997 and valued at $5.6 billion in its acquisition by Bain Capital, the company provides a unified platform covering student information systems, learning management, assessment, enrollment, attendance, HR, finance, and state compliance reporting.
profile.end_customers: PowerSchool's customers are K-12 school districts, charter schools, and state education agencies, including more than 90 of the top 100 U.S. districts by enrollment. The platform is used daily by school administrators, teachers, students, and parents for everything from grade tracking and attendance to enrollment management and compliance reporting.

profile.business_description: SimonMed is one of the nation's largest physician-owned outpatient radiology and diagnostic imaging providers, founded in 2003 and headquartered in Scottsdale, Arizona, with over 170 locations across 10 states. The company offers MRI, CT, 3D mammography, ultrasound, X-ray, and other imaging services, deploying approximately 200 board-certified subspecialty radiologists who use AI-enhanced diagnostic tools, and partnered with American Securities in a $600 million deal in 2021.
profile.end_customers: SimonMed's end-customers are patients referred for diagnostic imaging by their physicians, as well as the referring physicians and health systems that order imaging studies. The company emphasizes same-day appointments, convenient locations, and affordable pricing with a 98% patient satisfaction rate, primarily serving commercially insured, Medicare, and self-pay patients.

profile.end_customers: Squarespace primarily serves small business owners, entrepreneurs, freelancers, and creative professionals who need an accessible way to build and maintain a web presence. comps.rationale: Global energy drink leader. Provides valuation ceiling for functional beverage category, though stronger credit profile. Useful as upside reference.

comps.debt: $1.3B TL B S+225 due 2028; 4.375% Senior Notes due 2029 ($500M)
comps.rationale: Healthy hydration company (water delivery/dispensers). European-style health-focused beverage positioning. Provides pricing reference for branded functional beverage debt.

profile.business_description: Vitamin Well is a Swedish functional food and beverage company founded in 2006, offering health-oriented drinks, protein snacks, and energy beverages through its three core brands: Vitamin Well flavored water, Nocco energy drinks, and Barebells protein bars. Headquartered in Stockholm with approximately 650 million euros in revenue, the company distributes products across more than 40 markets worldwide.
profile.end_customers: Vitamin Well targets health-conscious and active consumers seeking premium, better-for-you beverage and snack alternatives. Its products are sold through grocery retailers, convenience stores, gyms, and health food outlets across Europe, North America, and Asia Pacific.

comps.name: RH (Restoration Hardware)
comps.debt: $2.0B TL B due 2028 (SOFR-based); convertible notes outstanding

profile.business_description: Walker Edison is an e-commerce-driven furniture company founded in 2006 in West Jordan, Utah, that designs, sources, and sells affordable, ready-to-assemble home furnishings. The company offers furniture for bedrooms, living rooms, dining areas, home offices, and outdoor spaces, leveraging data-driven design, global sourcing operations in China, Vietnam, and Brazil, and direct-to-consumer logistics.
profile.end_customers: Walker Edison targets value-conscious consumers shopping for affordable, modern furniture primarily through e-commerce channels including Amazon, Walmart.com, and the company's own website. Its products also reach consumers through home improvement retailers like The Home Depot, appealing to first-time homeowners, apartment renters, and budget-minded families furnishing their homes.

profile.end_customers: The primary end customers are homeowners undertaking window replacement and renovation projects, typically reached through direct-to-consumer sales channels. Home builders, remodeling contractors, and specialty window retailers also purchase the company's products for new construction and retrofit applications.

investments.industry: Asset based lending and fund finance
investments.origination_notes: Equity investment in both 2024 and 2025 portfolios; origination date uncertain

comps.name: Ares Capital Corporation
comps.debt: $750M 5.500% Senior Notes due 2030; also issued $2.4B of investment-grade bonds in 2025 at ~S+180bps
comps.rationale: Largest publicly traded BDC with ~$29B in assets, focused on direct lending to middle-market companies. Closest public comp to a specialty lending platform like Wingspire.

comps.name: Golub Capital BDC
comps.debt: Multiple unsecured notes outstanding; publicly traded BDC with ~$8B+ portfolio
comps.rationale: Middle-market direct lender focused on sponsor-backed companies, similar ABL/specialty lending profile to Wingspire's upper-middle-market focus.

comps.name: Blue Owl Capital Corporation
comps.debt: Multiple unsecured notes outstanding (3.400% due 2026, 4.700% due 2027, 7.950% due 2028, among others)
comps.rationale: OBDC itself is the parent BDC that owns Wingspire, and its own publicly traded unsecured notes provide the most direct benchmark for Wingspire's credit quality.

profile.business_description: Wingspire Capital is a diversified specialty finance company and portfolio company of Blue Owl Capital Corporation, providing senior secured asset-based loans (ABL) and term loans to U.S. middle-market companies. The company offers revolving credit lines and term loans ranging from $20 million to $200 million, secured by accounts receivable, inventory, equipment, real estate, and intellectual property, and has closed over $4.5 billion in financing commitments since inception.
profile.end_customers: Wingspire's primary end customers are middle-market companies across manufacturing, distribution, retail, logistics, automotive, energy, consumer products, hospitality, and business services that need asset-based working capital or acquisition financing. These borrowers typically cannot access traditional bank markets at competitive terms and benefit from Wingspire's flexible, collateral-based lending approach.

investments.sponsor_source: Wingspire is a controlled/affiliated portfolio company launched by Blue Owl Capital Corporation (OBDC) in 2019; it is a specialty finance platform, not a PE-backed LBO

profile.business_description: Wipfli Advisory is the tax and business consulting subsidiary of Wipfli, a top-20 national accounting and advisory firm with nearly 100 years of operating history, over 3,000 associates, 48 offices, and more than 54,000 clients. Backed by New Mountain Capital, the firm provides tax compliance, business consulting, digital transformation, outsourcing, risk advisory, and corporate finance services.
profile.end_customers: Wipfli's primary end customers are ambitious middle-market companies across manufacturing, construction, healthcare, financial services, nonprofit, and technology sectors seeking tax, consulting, and digital transformation services. The firm also serves community banks, credit unions, and tribal organizations requiring specialized regulatory compliance and advisory support.

profile.business_description: West Monroe is a management and technology consulting firm that blends traditional business strategy consulting with deep technical implementation capabilities, assembling multidisciplinary teams to deliver end-to-end digital transformation solutions. The firm provides consulting across operations, product engineering, and creative services with approximately 2,100 employees.
profile.end_customers: West Monroe's end-customers are mid-market and large enterprises across industries including private equity firms (portfolio company diligence and transformation), energy and utilities, financial services, healthcare, and software/technology companies. The firm also serves consumer and industrial products companies and retail organizations seeking operational improvement and digital modernization.

comps.rationale: Specialty EHR for behavioral health. GI Partners/TA Associates-backed. Similar scale and business model (specialty-focused EHR platform). Historically BSL loans traded.

profile.business_description: WP Irving Co-Invest is a co-investment vehicle associated with the investment in Intelerad Medical Systems, the global leader in cloud-based PACS and enterprise medical imaging software serving nearly 2,500 healthcare organizations worldwide. Intelerad's IntelePACS platform provides radiology departments with high-volume diagnostic image management, viewing, and reporting, and GE HealthCare has announced a $2.3 billion acquisition of the company.
profile.end_customers: The underlying company, Intelerad, serves radiology departments at hospitals, health systems, and independent imaging centers that need enterprise-scale medical image archiving, distribution, and clinical viewing tools. The platform also supports teleradiology practices and multi-site radiology networks requiring cloud-based image access across locations.

The company targets middle-to-upper-income homeowners in its metropolitan markets for both emergency repair calls and planned system replacements.

The company manufactures formulations designed for specific surfaces and applications -- stainless steel, granite, glass, leather, and medical equipment -- serving both consumer and commercial cleaning segments.

comps.name: Halozyme Therapeutics
comps.debt: $1.3B 1.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2028
comps.rationale: Royalty/milestone-driven biotech with significant traded convertible debt. Similar royalty-based revenue model from partnered drug programs.

profile.business_description: XOMA Royalty (NASDAQ: XOMA) is a biotech royalty aggregator that acquires rights to future milestone and royalty payments from pre-commercial therapeutic candidates being developed by pharmaceutical and biotech companies. The company has a portfolio of over 120 assets across 11+ therapeutic categories, with 42 assets partnered with large-cap pharma companies, leveraging its 35-year history and $1 billion of prior antibody R&D investment to evaluate assets.
profile.end_customers: XOMA's direct customers are early-to-mid-stage biotechnology companies that sell their royalty and milestone rights in exchange for non-dilutive, non-recourse funding to advance their internal drug development programs. The company's economic returns ultimately depend on pharmaceutical and biotech companies successfully developing and commercializing the underlying drug candidates through regulatory approval and market adoption.

comps.rationale: Large PE-backed enterprise SaaS (HCM) with both syndicated loans and high-yield bonds trading in secondary markets. Provides deep liquidity and pricing transparency for comparable software credit.

profile.business_description: Zendesk provides cloud-based customer service and engagement software that enables businesses to manage support interactions across email, chat, phone, social media, and messaging channels from a unified agent workspace. The platform incorporates AI agents and automation to handle routine inquiries, along with self-service knowledge bases, ticketing, and analytics.
profile.end_customers: Zendesk serves over 100,000 businesses across industries and in over 30 languages, ranging from small businesses to large enterprises that need to manage customer support operations. Its customers span virtually every industry including technology, retail, financial services, healthcare, and media, with both B2B and B2C companies using it to manage customer and employee service interactions.

investments.sponsor_source: Hellman & Friedman and Permira led consortium that took Zendesk private for $10.2B ($77.50/share) in November 2022

profile.business_description: Zoro TopCo, L.P. is the holding company structure created by Hellman & Friedman and Permira for the $10.2 billion take-private acquisition of Zendesk in 2022. Its platform is used by customer service teams, sales organizations, and internal IT helpdesks across virtually every industry including technology, e-commerce, financial services, media, and healthcare.
